ops.title: Fleet design system: CSS token architecture and styling philosophy
ops.body:
# Fleet design system (gd-build fleet-theme.css)

Source: `tools/gd-build/gd_build/assets/fleet-theme.css`, materialized to consumer repos at `docs/assets/.gd-build/fleet-theme.css` by `gd_build/fleet_assets.py` (titles.py pattern). Wire-up: the `css:` config entry from `fleet_assets.quarto_config_entries()` merges at the `_write_quarto_yml` seam — copy `CSS_DEST` into the Quarto staging root, reference by basename (great-docs' own site.css mechanism, core.py:284-287 + 11847-11849). Loads AFTER the compiled flatly + great-docs.scss theme, so equal-specificity rules win by order.

## Token architecture

- All fleet colors route through `--fleet-*` tokens declared on `:root` (light) and `html.quarto-dark, body.quarto-dark` (dark — matches upstream's dark selector convention).
- `--fleet-link: var(--gd-accent, <fallback>)` — repo accents flow in via great-docs' injected `--gd-accent` (per-mode, from `accent_color` in great-docs.yml). Fallbacks are WCAG-verified: light `#2563eb` 5.17:1 on white, dark `#8ea9ff` 7.68:1 on `#1a1a1a`.
- The CSS also overrides `--bs-link-color` and `--gd-link-color` so Bootstrap/upstream link paths inherit the same token. This fixes the measured flatly defect: `#18bc9c` links on white = 2.41:1.
- Hovers derive via `color-mix(in oklab, var(--fleet-link) 78%, black|white)`.

## Hard constraints

- **WCAG AA (≥4.5:1) for every color pair** — fixed hexes verified arithmetically at authoring time; per-repo accents guaranteed by the fleet palette rule: light accent ≥4.5:1 on white, dark accent ≥4.5:1 on `#1a1a1a` AND ideally on code bg `#2a2a2a` (cc-steer's `#ef5350` fails the code-bg check at 4.12:1 — refinement `#ef6b62`).
- **Navbar/sidebar colors stay untouched** — great-docs picks navbar text via APCA against `navbar_color`; fleet link rules are scoped with `:where(main.content, #quarto-document-content, .content) a:not(.btn):not(.nav-link):not(.sidebar-item-text)`.
- **No structural layout changes** — no display:none content hiding; only measure (50rem content cap ≥992px, homepage excluded via `body:not(.gd-homepage)`), spacing, color.
- **Palette picks derive from the repo mascot** (`docs/assets/logo.png`): navbar = deep tone from the mascot with white text ≥4.5:1; accent = mascot hue darkened/lightened per mode until AA passes. Seed guidance lives in the repo-bootstrap great-docs-repo.fragment.yml TODO.

## Fragment composition gotcha

cc-guides composes great-docs.yml by **concatenating** fragments (fleet fragment appended after repo-local); duplicate top-level keys silently resolve last-wins under PyYAML. So the fleet fragment (`plugin/guides/yml/great-docs-fleet.yml`) must never carry keys any repo fragment sets (`hero:`, `css:`, `accent_color:` are off-limits; `freeze: auto` was verified collision-free). Hero-logo suppression must be a gd-build config patch, not a fleet fragment key.
ops.when: before modifying docs styling, theme colors, or fleet design tokens
